title: Lesson 159: (Grammar) - had better; had better not

intro_cards.title: Advice: ほうがいい
intro_cards.body:
ほうがいい means 'it is better to ~' — giving advice.

With た-form: advice to DO something:
• 早く寝たほうがいい。(You should sleep early.)
• 医者に行ったほうがいいよ。(You should see a doctor.)

With ない-form: advice NOT to do:
• そんなに食べないほうがいい。(You shouldn't eat so much.)
• 無理しないほうがいいよ。(You shouldn't overdo it.)

Note: たほうがいい suggests something the listener hasn't done yet.
When they HAVE done it: てよかった (glad you did).
